# SNOBOL4-tiny — Bootstrap Artifacts

This directory preserves historically significant build outputs.
These are **not** regenerated source — they are snapshots of
compiler output at key milestones.

---

## `beauty_full_first_clean.c`

**What**: The first C file generated by `snoc` from `beauty.sno`
(with all 19 `-INCLUDE` libraries resolved via `snobol4_inc.c`)
that compiles with **zero gcc errors**.

**When**: Session 33, 2026-03-12.

**Stats**: 10,543 lines of C. md5: `f9e54c5877b519b2efbbfbf558ddd7ad`

**Why it matters**: This is the direct output of SNOBOL4-tiny's
`snoc` compiler translating a 801-line SNOBOL4 program (plus 19
included library files) to a single flat C translation unit.
It is the artifact that proved the compiler works end-to-end.

**The design fix that unlocked it** (Session 33):
`DEFINE('bVisit(x,fnc)i', 'bVisit_')` — the two-argument form of
DEFINE where the second argument is an explicit entry label distinct
from the function name. Prior to this fix, `bVisit_`'s body was
emitted inline in `main()` instead of inside `_sno_fn_bVisit()`,
causing an early exit before `main00` was ever reached. Fix: added
`entry_label` field to `FnDef`; `body_starts` search now uses
`entry_label` when present; `is_body_boundary` and `fn_by_label`
both check `entry_label`. See `src/snoc/emit.c`.

**Build command that produced it**:
```bash
snoc beauty.sno -I $INC > beauty_full_first_clean.c
gcc -O0 -g beauty_full_first_clean.c \
    src/runtime/snobol4/snobol4.c \
    src/runtime/snobol4/snobol4_inc.c \
    src/runtime/snobol4/snobol4_pattern.c \
    src/runtime/engine.c \
    -Isrc/runtime/snobol4 -Isrc/runtime \
    -lgc -lm -w -o beauty_full_bin
# → 0 errors, binary links
```
